steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student wants to emphasize a similarity in how people in Bangkok and Chiang Mai celebrate Songkran. The notes show a clear parallel: in Bangkok, thousands gather along Silom Road for a water fight; in Chiang Mai, thousands gather at the Tha Phae Gate for a water fight. The correct answer must highlight this shared pattern.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Each April, people in Thailand celebrate Songkran, the traditional Thai New Year.': Gives only general background about Songkran in Thailand — says nothing specific about Bangkok or Chiang Mai. ✗
  • Choice B — 'The largest water fight in Bangkok takes place along a city street, whereas the largest water fight in Chiang Mai takes place at a historical monument.': Uses "whereas" to emphasize a difference in venue, not a similarity. ✗
  • Choice C — 'In both Bangkok and Chiang Mai, thousands gather to celebrate Songkran with water fights.': Directly highlights the parallel: both cities draw thousands of people to celebrate with water fights. ✓
  • Choice D — 'People in both Bangkok and Chiang Mai celebrate Songkran, but they don't do so in exactly the same way.': Explicitly states the celebrations are not the same — the opposite of what the goal requires. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
